text: Perineal abdomen resection, laparoscopic due to rectal cancer. The case of a woman of fifty-nine years, without oncological or family history of importance. Time of illness of eighteen months, characterized by abdominal pain, rectorrhagia and weight loss of five kilos. According to colonoscopy, at two centimeters of anal margin, a proliferative circumferential lesion with an extension of six centimeters, friable to the biopsy. With pathological result adenocarcinoma well differentiated.

The patient is positioned in the dorsal cubicle in the lithotomy position. Four trocars are inserted: a 10mm trocar in the umbilical region, two 5mm trocars in the right hypochondriac and left flank, and a 12mm trocar in the right iliac pit.

The surgeon performs a complete intra-abdominal evaluation, visually inspecting for metastasis and peritoneal implants. The abdominal cavity is washed, and a sample is collected for cytological evaluation. The promontory area is identified, and dissection of the posterior peritoneum begins.

The surgeon identifies the incision plane and proceeds cephalically to locate the inferior mesenteric vessels. Two hemologs are placed on the proximal part of the inferior mesenteric artery, and a distal clip is applied.

The surgeon continues dissecting the sigmoid mesocolon, using traction and counter-traction maneuvers to release the retroperitoneum. The lateral approach to the sigmoid colon is initiated through the ethole phase for complete release.

The surgeon uses a glass sealing clip to perform the section of the sigmoid mesocolon. The intestinal section area is identified at the colosigmoideal junction, where a 60mm linear stapler is applied to complete the section.

The surgeon performs a complete dissection of the rectum through the endopelvic fascia, preserving the genitourinary neurovascular plexus. The rectal mesorectal dissection is completed, followed by a perineal approach for the transanal extraction of the surgical specimen.
A silicone drainage tube is placed in the pelvic pit, exiting through the right contraverture. The surgeon creates a colostomy in the descending colon, which exits through the left contraverture.
